codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly threads existing commit metadata from Rust ProjectState through ProjectStatus to the frontend ProjectStatus type, which matches the sync beacon context's expected camelCase fields.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-fullstack skill currently requires browser verification even for backend/frontend data-plumbing changes that do not alter UI behavior, which did not match this task's needs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations notes browser verification was skipped because the feature only changed serialized types, and .factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md step 6 still mandates starting the frontend and manual browser checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-fullstack skill mandates test-first Rust changes, but this feature was a minimal pass-through of already existing ProjectState fields and the worker reasonably relied on existing suite coverage instead of adding a new targeted test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations cites SKILL.md step 3, while the code already had ProjectState commit metadata in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/git.rs:61-73 and sync beacon serialization coverage in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs:2879-2908.

summary: Pass. The diff adds the missing commit metadata fields to Rust ProjectStatus, forwards them in get_all_project_statuses with safe defaults for error cases, and extends the frontend ProjectStatus interface so sync-beacon-context can populate beacon payloads with real commit metadata. No blocking or non-blocking code issues found; only skill-procedure gaps were noted.
